CREATE TABLE more_dates (start_date DATE, end_date DATE);
INSERT INTO more_dates VALUES (DATE '1992-01-01', DATE '1992-12-31'), (DATE '1992-06-01', DATE '1992-06-15');
SELECT start_date, end_date, greatest(start_date, end_date) AS max_date FROM more_dates;
CREATE TABLE complex_dates (id INT, date1 DATE, date2 DATE);
INSERT INTO complex_dates VALUES (1, DATE '1995-01-01', DATE '1996-01-01'), (2, DATE '1994-06-01', DATE '1994-05-01');
SELECT id, greatest(date1, date2) AS max_date FROM complex_dates WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, greatest(subquery.date_1, subquery.date_2) AS max_date FROM (SELECT DATE '1990-01-01' AS date_1, DATE '1995-12-31' AS date_2) AS subquery;
CREATE TABLE joined_dates (id INT, date_entry DATE);
INSERT INTO joined_dates VALUES (1, DATE '2000-01-01'), (2, DATE '1999-12-31');
SELECT j.id, greatest(j.date_entry, d.date_col) AS latest_date FROM joined_dates j JOIN dates_table d ON j.id = 1;
SELECT greatest(greatest(DATE '2020-01-01', DATE '2019-12-31'), DATE '2021-01-01') AS ultimate_date;
